w20: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring multiple NVIDIA A100 GPUs and substantial VRAM, delivering top-tier performance for large language model inference and demanding parallel workloads.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g: Accelerator-optimized with 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 160GB VRAM, and 48 vCPUs for demanding AI/ML and HPC workloads.
c200: Google Cloud Platform's a2-highgpu-4g offers extreme acceleration. Featuring four NVIDIA A100 GPUs with 160GB VRAM, 48 x86_64 vCPUs, and 340GB RAM, it's optimized for intensive AI, machine learning, and HPC workloads.
w150: Google Cloud Platform's a2-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server, featuring an x86_64 architecture with 48 virtual CPUs across 24 dedicated physical cores, operating at 2.2 GHz, and provisioned with 340 GB of system memory. The primary computational power derives from four NVIDIA A100 GPUs, collectively offering 160 GB of total VRAM. This configuration does not include local storage. Performance analysis indicates average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, with single-core CPU tasks and specific compression operations ranking in the bottom 25%. However, the instance excels in GPU-intensive tasks, achieving top-tier LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation across various model sizes, from 135M to 70B parameters. This server is engineered for deep learning training, complex AI/ML model inference, high-performance computing simulations, and large-scale data science jobs requiring significant GPU acceleration.
